justification: All pass criteria met: 6 failure modes identified (exceeds 5 minimum); phantom charge risk explicitly covered in FM3 (double charge on retry) and FM5 (partial failure where payment succeeds but order status not updated); fire-and-forget notification weakness thoroughly addressed with transactional outbox pattern replacing lossy HTTP; circuit breaker and timeout budget patterns recommended with bulkhead isolation; idempotency keys specified end-to-end with Stripe Idempotency-Key header forwarding; trade-offs for added complexity acknowledged across 4 detailed entries covering circuit breakers, outbox pattern, idempotency keys, and timeout budgets.

observations.text: Exceptional failure mode coverage — 6 distinct modes including the subtle backpressure issue (FM6) beyond the expected 5
Phantom charge addressed from two angles: retry-induced double charge (FM3 via idempotency keys) and crash-induced orphaned charge (FM5 via write-ahead and reconciliation)
Fire-and-forget correctly identified as an architectural issue requiring a pattern change (transactional outbox), not a local fix — matches expected behavior
Explicitly warns against retrying without idempotency keys in pitfalls_to_avoid — directly avoids a key failure mode from the rubric
Implementation guidance is phased and specific with concrete patterns (circuit breaker, bulkhead, outbox, reconciliation job)
Trade-offs are well-structured with alternatives_considered, showing genuine architectural reasoning rather than just listing patterns
Distinguishes between transient failures (worth retrying with circuit breaker) and permanent failures (fail fast) via the circuit breaker state machine description
Retry parameters not fully specified — mentions 3-5s timeouts but lacks the explicit backoff schedule (e.g., 100ms/500ms/1000ms) called for in the expected behavior

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) 7+ failure modes identified (Payment Service unavailable, Stripe outage/cascade, timeout ambiguity/phantom charge, silent notification loss, orphaned payment, retry storm, outbox growth). (2) Phantom charge risk explicitly identified in both 'orphaned payment' and 'timeout ambiguity' risks with correct mitigation (idempotency keys + reconciliation, never assume failure on timeout). (3) Fire-and-forget weakness thoroughly addressed with Transactional Outbox replacement. (4) Circuit breaker and timeout patterns recommended with specific placement guidance and layered values (5s/8s/10s deadline propagation). (5) Idempotency keys specified at every boundary with Stripe forwarding. (6) Trade-offs for added complexity acknowledged across three detailed entries.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough failure mode enumeration — goes beyond the 5 expected to include retry storms and outbox growth
Correctly identifies that timeout ≠ failure and warns against assuming failure on timeout — this is the key phantom charge insight
Provides specific implementation guidance with retry parameters, timeout layering, and phased rollout plan
Bulkhead pattern included as additional resilience layer beyond the expected circuit breaker and timeout
Pitfalls section explicitly warns against retrying without idempotency keys and against placing circuit breaker in the wrong service
Transactional Outbox recommendation correctly identifies the dual-write problem that a naive message broker integration would recreate
Compensation/saga pattern mentioned for the payment-succeeds-but-order-crashes scenario
Distinguishes transient vs permanent failures implicitly through circuit breaker (permanent/sustained) vs retry with backoff (transient)

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: 9 failure modes identified (well above 5 minimum), phantom charge risk explicitly covered (FM3 double-charge and FM4 inconsistent state), fire-and-forget notification weakness addressed with Transactional Outbox pattern, circuit breaker and timeout patterns recommended with specific placement, idempotency keys specified end-to-end (Order→Payment→Stripe), and trade-offs for added complexity thoroughly acknowledged across three detailed trade-off entries.

observations.text: Exceptional failure mode coverage — 9 distinct modes including subtle ones like thundering herd on recovery and outbox relay lag
Phantom charge addressed from two angles: idempotency keys for retry safety (FM3) and compensating transactions for crash-after-payment (FM4)
Fire-and-forget replacement is thorough — Transactional Outbox with DLQ, not just adding retries to HTTP
Implementation guidance includes specific retry parameters, phased rollout, and concrete pitfalls-to-avoid list
Correctly distinguishes transient vs permanent failures and recommends different patterns for each
Trade-offs are genuinely balanced — acknowledges that sync Order→Payment coupling is intentionally kept for UX reasons rather than dogmatically decoupling everything
Identifies that distributed transactions (2PC) should be avoided in favor of saga/compensation — shows architectural maturity
